policy: The roadmap acceptance criteria are tracked as evidence-backed status records. Human signoff, public package upload, non-maintainer adoption, and non-Codex/reviewer-supplied hardness evidence must remain pending until real external events are recorded.

status_definitions.proven: Current repository, CI, release, or published-site evidence directly supports the criterion.
status_definitions.partial: A substantial internal portion is complete, but the criterion includes a future human, package-index, or external-event component.
status_definitions.pending_human: The criterion requires named human maintainer action that Codex cannot perform.
status_definitions.pending_package_index: The criterion requires a public package-index upload that a named maintainer must perform.
status_definitions.pending_external: The criterion requires non-maintainer or non-Codex evidence that is not yet accepted.

open_gates.blocker: Named maintainer review, date, decision, and correction outcome must be recorded before human audit or canonical promotion can be claimed.
TestPyPI/PyPI upload must be performed by a named maintainer before public package-index smoke checks can run.
At least three non-maintainer reports must be accepted and published with friction/failure fields intact.
At least one additional non-Codex or reviewer-supplied hardness surface must be validated and accepted before cross-surface Bench v4 hardness claims are complete.

criteria.label: Public site teaches the theory clearly.
Main book is readable and coherent.
Pocket guide is useful as a daily reference.
Stacked-spells system is integrated and validated.
Full lexicon is structured, navigable, and semantically reviewed.
Six initial spells are reusable templates.
Six initial stacks are reusable workflows.
Spells and stacks have schemas.
Validation, tests, render, and link audit run in CI.
Quarto publication is automated.
Executable fixtures demonstrate Proof by Difference for field spells.
Recorded runs show weak-vs-repaired deltas, scores, variance, and non-wins.
Project can accept contributions without losing structure.
Project has versioned releases.
User can move from vague request to structured spell to verified result.
Team can move from one-off prompt to reusable stack to versioned practice.
Homepage demonstrates practical value before jargon.
Every field spell has copyable and raw template forms.
Fifty major canon entries are authored and reviewed.
Three hundred pocket canon entries are authored and reviewed.
All 1,645 lexicon entries have authored fields and sense guidance where needed.
Master-lexicon house pages are complete and navigable.
Tool-native exports are generated and link to seals.
Release-gate stack is instantiated by the repository release process.
Seal stability is tested and seal changes are documented.
Jailbreaks and prompt injection are covered defensively.
Warded-spell trust-boundary schema extension exists.
Jailbreak-resilience spell and AI red-team stack exist.
Jailbreak-resilience bench has harmless fixtures, scoring, transcripts, and utility scoring.
No operational external jailbreak corpus is vendored by default.
Lexicon distinguishes generated draft text from reviewed semantic canon.
Template-shaped lexicon prose cannot pass as reviewed or canonical.
Major and pocket runes have reviewed force, shadow, examples, and prompt-use guidance.
Benchmark layer supports declared surfaces and manual imports.
Benchmark results include deterministic checks, metadata, variance, and limitations.
Adversarial harness covers mediation, retrieval taint, scope creep, drift, canary redaction, and overrefusal.
Exported assets ship with manifest, checksums, schema versions, source IDs, and seals.
CLI/package can validate and seal user-owned spells outside source data.
Generator is split into maintainable modules with deterministic tests.
Diagram placeholders are replaced by generated visual instruments.
Task chooser maps real work to spell, stack, template, and verification path.
Adoption, canon correction, and reviewer-run import paths are published without fabricating evidence.
Field-spell evaluation includes clean and trap tiers.
Execution-graded cases preserve command, streams, exit code, timeout, and grader version.
Benchmark pages separate reviewability, outcome, execution, variance, and limitations.
At least two model/tool surfaces are recorded or imported for field-spell bench.
Every jailbreak-resilience case has baseline and warded variants.
Adversarial matrix includes baseline failure and warded resistance/utility/audit reporting.
Surface comparison pages show deltas without hiding ties, losses, or failures.
Semantic canon has a validated promotion ladder.
Per-house semantic progress board exists and two houses are reviewed.
Reviewed lexicon count is at least 450.
Claude Code exports are generated, validated, checksummed, bundled, and installable.
Spell and stack diagrams are data-driven and non-placeholder.
Release bundles are produced and attached to GitHub Releases.
Calibration artifacts are separated from model-run evidence.
Every evidence row carries evidence class and provenance.
Calibration rows cannot count as independent model/tool surfaces.
Field-spell bench includes a real second model/tool surface beyond original Codex.
Surface adapters share normalized metadata contract.
Model artifacts can be extracted, sandboxed, and execution-graded safely.
Trap-tier field-spell cases include real model outputs and preserved transcripts.
At least two field-spell cases grade model-produced artifacts by execution.
Jailbreak-resilience A/B comparisons include real model runs.
At least one adversarial baseline failure is model-produced.
Evidence dashboard links from summaries to prompts, transcripts, artifacts, fixtures, scores, and limitations.
Live-site smoke checks verify high-value pages and downloads after deployment.
Release assets are downloadable and checksum-verifiable after publication.
First two reviewed lexicon houses have human audit records and correction outcomes.
blockers: Named human audit records and correction outcomes have not been recorded.

criteria.label: Canonical rune promotion is backed by usage evidence and reviewer signoff.
blockers: Canonical count must remain zero until named maintainer signoff exists.

criteria.label: Usage graph links runes to spells, stacks, benchmarks, adoption reports, and accepted evidence.
CLI is installable from a public package index and passes package-install smoke tests.
blockers: No public package-index upload has been performed by a named maintainer.

criteria.label: Public package versioning is tied to evidence milestones.
At least three non-maintainer adoption or reviewer reports are accepted and published.
blockers: No accepted non-maintainer external adoption reports are published yet.

criteria.label: Project preserves ties, losses, overrefusals, failures, and null results.
Evaluation pages report deltas per surface, tier, variant, and repetition cell.
Structural rubric is renamed to reviewability score.
Bench v4 includes five named hardness rungs.
At least two hardness rungs include executable fixtures, artifacts, and cross-surface results.
blockers: Current accepted Bench v4 model-surface evidence is Codex-only; non-Codex or reviewer-supplied accepted hardness evidence is still pending.

criteria.label: Ward-science pages include real baseline/warded A/B on both primary surfaces for original attack shapes.
At least one ward-limb ablation is published with effect/non-effect attribution.
Additional defanged attack shapes cover six named morphologies.
Resistance-versus-utility reporting prevents blanket refusal from scoring as complete success.
Canon-review queue is generated from usage evidence and bounded for human review.
Canonical entries require reviewed status, usage evidence, no blocker, and named signoff.
One-step local installs exist for Claude Code and Cursor and are tested under repo-local tmp.
Adoption-report generation produces schema-valid records without counting dogfood as external adoption.
Package-index release materials are ready and package-index smoke checks are added once upload exists.
blockers: Post-upload package-index smoke checks cannot run until the human package-index upload exists.

criteria.label: Scratch output policy keeps project commands, docs, tests, and examples under repo-local tmp.
Methods write-up is generated from recorded evidence and foregrounds null/tie/task-dependent results.
